questions.question: Draw a line to match each object. Left: ☆ ☆ ☆. Right: ○ ○ ○. Match one star to one circle.
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: One-to-One Correspondence - Match objects one-to-one

questions.question: Circle the objects that are big. (Images: big ball, small ball, big book, small pencil)
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Classification - Compare objects by one property

questions.question: Arrange these numbers in order from smallest to biggest: 1, 3, 2
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Seriation - Arrange 2-3 objects by shape, size, order

questions.question: Look at the group: Apple, Banana, Chair, Orange. Which one does not belong?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Odd One Out - Identify object that does not belong based on shape, size, color, or category

questions.question: Match each shape to its name: Left: ○ △ □, Right: Circle Triangle Square
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Matching - Match objects, shapes, quantities, and relationships

questions.question: Count the apples: 🍎🍎🍎🍎🍎 How many apples?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Counting to 5 - Count up to 5 objects

questions.question: Look at the number 4. Which number is this? (1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Numeral Recognition (1-5) - Identify numerals up to 5

questions.question: If you have 5 balloons and all fly away, how many balloons are left?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Zero Awareness - Understand absence of objects

questions.question: Which group has MORE? Left: ☆☆ (2 stars), Right: ☆☆☆☆☆ (5 stars)
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Comparing Numbers (Up to 5) - Compare numbers using more than, less than

questions.question: Count forward: 1, 2, __, 4, 5. What number is missing?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Forward and Backward Counting (To 5) - Count forward and backward up to 5

questions.question: Write the number name for 3.
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Number Names (1-5) - Identify number names one, two, three, four, five

questions.question: Show 3 fingers. How many fingers are you showing?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Finger Gesture Counting - Connect finger representations to quantities up to 5

questions.question: What number comes AFTER 3 in the sequence: 1, 2, 3, _?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: After, Between, Before (1-5) - Understand number sequence and position within 1-5

questions.question: Sort these into two groups: red ball, blue ball, red book, blue book. How many objects are red?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Classification (Multiple Factors) - Compare objects using three factors (shape, color, size)

questions.question: Arrange by height: short plant, tall tree, medium bush, very tall building (write 1, 2, 3, 4)
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Seriation (4-5 Objects) - Arrange 4-5 objects or pictures, sequence events

questions.question: Count the stars: ☆☆☆☆☆☆☆☆☆☆ How many stars?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Counting to 10 - Count up to 10 objects

questions.question: Count forward from 6 to 9.
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Forward and Backward Counting (To 9) - Count forward and backward up to 9

questions.question: Write the number 7 in the box.
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Numeral Writing (1-9) - Identify and write numerals up to 9

questions.question: Write the number name for 7.
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Number Names (1-10) - Identify number names for numbers 1 to 10

questions.question: You have 7 toys. You give away all 7. How many toys do you have now?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Understanding Zero - Understand reduction to zero

questions.question: Which is bigger: 8 or 3?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Comparing Numbers (Up to 10) - Compare numbers up to 10

questions.question: Look at these: red square, blue circle, red triangle, blue square. How many red shapes are there?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Classification (Class 1 Level) - Compare and classify objects using multiple factors with position words

questions.question: Arrange these numbers from smallest to largest: 7, 3, 9, 5, 11, 1
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Seriation (More than 5 Objects) - Arrange more than 5 objects based on criteria

questions.question: Count: ☆☆☆☆☆☆☆☆☆☆☆☆☆☆☆☆☆☆☆☆ How many stars? (20)
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Counting to 20 - Count objects up to 20 using pictures and concrete objects

questions.question: Count backward from 20: 20, 19, 18, __, 16
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Forward and Backward Counting (To 20) - Count forward and backward up to 20 from any number

questions.question: Write the number eighty-seven.
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Number Reading and Writing (1-99) - Read and write numerals up to 99
Covers subtopic: Understanding Zero (Class 1) - Understand the concept of zero

questions.question: Compare: 14 _ 17. Write >, < or =
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Comparing Numbers (Up to 20) - Compare numbers using greater than and less than vocabulary

questions.question: How many tens and ones in 15?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Tens and Ones (11-30) - Identify tens and ones using bundles, blocks, or beads for numbers 11-30

questions.question: Count and write the number: (Picture of 23 objects) How many?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Numbers 11-30 - Count, read, write, and identify number names for numbers 11-30

questions.question: Count 12 apples. Trace and write the number 12.
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Counting with Tracing - Match quantities with numerals through counting and tracing activities

questions.question: What number comes AFTER 18? 18, __
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: After, Between, Before (11-30) - Understand after, before, and between for numbers 11-30

questions.question: Arrange in ascending order: 25, 12, 30, 18
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Ordering (1-30) - Arrange numbers up to 30 in ascending and descending order

questions.question: Write the number that comes between 34 and 36.
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Numbers 31-50 - Write, sequence, and identify number names for numbers 31-50

questions.question: Fill the missing numbers: 2, 4, __, 8, 10 (skip count by 2s)
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Skip Counting (2s and 3s) - Skip count by 2s and 3s using frog jump patterns

questions.question: Compare: 34 _ 43. Write >, < or =
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Comparing Numbers (1-50) - Compare numbers up to 50 using greater than (>), less than (<), equal (=) symbols

questions.question: Arrange in descending order: 45, 23, 38, 50
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Ordering (1-50) - Arrange numbers up to 50 in ascending and descending order

questions.question: In the number 46, how many tens and how many ones?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Place Value (2-Digit) - Represent 2-digit numbers as tens and ones, understand place value

questions.question: Write the number that comes after 99.
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Numbers up to 100 - Count, read, write, and sequence numbers up to 100

questions.question: Compare: 67 _ 76. Write >, < or =
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Comparing Numbers (Within 100) - Compare numbers up to 100 using greater than, less than, equal symbols

questions.question: Arrange in ascending order: 45, 23, 67, 12
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Ordering Numbers (Within 100) - Arrange numbers up to 100 in ascending and descending order

questions.question: Count 7 objects using tally marks.
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Tally Marks - Count and represent quantities using tally marks

questions.question: In a race, Rahul came first, Seema came second, and Amit came third. Who came FIRST?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Ordinal Positions (1st-10th) - Identify and write ordinal numbers first through tenth

questions.question: If you have 2 apples and your friend gives you 1 more, how many apples do you have?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Early Addition Awareness - Combining groups increases quantity

questions.question: Add: ★★ (2 stars) + ★★★ (3 stars) = ?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Combining Groups (To 5) - Combine groups up to 5 objects

questions.question: You have 4 candies. You eat 1. How many are left?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Taking Away - Take objects from a collection and recount

questions.question: Add: ★★★ (3 stars) + ★★★★ (4 stars) = ?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Combining Groups (To 9) - Combine groups up to 9 objects

questions.question: There are 8 birds on a tree. 3 fly away. How many birds are still on the tree?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Taking Away (From 9) - Remove objects and recount

questions.question: What is 9 + 5?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Addition (Up to 18) - Construct addition facts up to 18 using concrete objects

questions.question: Add: 14 + 5 = ?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Addition (Up to 30) - Add numbers up to 30 without carrying

questions.question: What is 7 - 3?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Subtraction (Up to 9) - Construct subtraction facts up to 9

questions.question: Subtract: 23 - 4 = ?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Subtraction (Up to 30) - Subtract numbers up to 30 without borrowing

questions.question: If 8 + 5 = 13, what is 13 - 5?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Addition and Subtraction Relationship - Understand the relationship between addition and subtraction

questions.question: How many legs do 3 chairs have? (Each chair has 4 legs) 4 + 4 + 4 = ?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Repeated Addition - Develop strategies for repeated addition up to 10

questions.question: Add: 47 + 25 = ?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Addition (Up to 99, With Carry) - Add 2-digit numbers with carrying

questions.question: Subtract: 63 - 28 = ?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Subtraction (Up to 99, With Borrow) - Subtract 2-digit numbers with borrowing

questions.question: There are 3 groups of 4 apples each. How many apples in total? 3 x 4 = ?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Multiplication (Repeated Addition) - Multiplication as repeated addition, equal grouping, tables 2, 3, 4

questions.question: Which word is related to money? (Pen, Rupee, Book, Chair)
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Money Vocabulary - Learn money-related words

questions.question: Which of these is a coin? (Rs 10 note, Rs 5 coin, Rs 100 note, Rs 20 note)
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Indian Coins - Identify Indian coins

questions.question: What is the value of this note? (Image of Rs 10 note)
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Indian Currency Notes - Identify Indian currency notes

questions.question: Show Rs 15 using notes and coins. How many Rs 10 notes and Rs 5 coins?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Representing Money - Represent amounts up to ₹20 using notes and coins

questions.question: Which is LONGER? (Picture of a pencil vs a crayon)
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Length Vocabulary - Long and short

questions.question: Which is HEAVIER? (Picture of a book vs a feather)
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Weight Vocabulary - Heavy and light

questions.question: Which glass is FULL? (Picture: one glass full, one empty)
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Capacity Vocabulary - Full and empty

questions.question: Who is TALLER? (Picture: a child and a giraffe)
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Length Comparison - Longer, shorter, taller

questions.question: Which is HEAVIER? An elephant or a mouse?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Weight Comparison - Heavier, lighter

questions.question: Which bottle holds MORE water? (Big bottle vs small bottle)
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Capacity Activities - Capacity through stories

questions.question: Look at three ribbons: Red (long), Blue (longer), Green (longest). Which is the LONGEST?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Length Comparison (Extended) - Longest, shortest, tallest

questions.question: Among a feather, a book, and a table, which is the HEAVIEST?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Weight Comparison (Extended) - Heaviest, lightest

questions.question: Which jar can hold the most water? (Small cup, Medium mug, Large bucket)
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Capacity Comparison - Compare capacities of containers

questions.question: How many paper clips long is this pencil? (Image: pencil = 6 paper clips)
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Length (Non-standard Units) - Estimate and measure short lengths using non-standard units

questions.question: Which is heavier: a book, a pencil, or a table?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Weight (Non-standard) - Compare three objects by weight

questions.question: How many cups of water can fill this jug? (Image: jug = 5 cups)
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Capacity (Non-standard Units) - Estimate and measure capacity using non-standard units

questions.question: Is ice cream hot or cold?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Temperature - Use hot and cold vocabulary

questions.question: A pencil is 15 cm long. A book is 30 cm long. What is longer?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Standard Measurement - Measure and compare length, weight, capacity using cm, m, g, kg, litres

questions.question: Which shape is a ball? (Circle, Square, Triangle, Rectangle)
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Basic Shapes - Ball, box, cap, cone

questions.question: A dice has corners. How many corners does a cube have?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Physical Features - Ball rolls, box has corners

questions.question: How many sides does a square have?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: 2D Shapes - Circle, square, triangle, rectangle

questions.question: Name a 3D shape that has no corners.
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: 3D Shapes (Class 1) - Identify and describe 3D shapes with faces, corners, surfaces

questions.question: If you cut an apple into 2 equal parts, each part is called _____
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Fraction Vocabulary - Half vocabulary

questions.question: If you fold a paper once and cut, how many equal parts do you get?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Half - Identify half and whole using paper folding

questions.question: Clap your hands 3 times. Tap your feet 3 times. Repeat. What comes next after clap, clap, clap?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Repeating Patterns - Clapping, clicking, tapping

questions.question: Complete the pattern: ○△○△○_
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Pattern Extension - Identify and extend patterns

questions.question: Draw the next shape in the pattern: △□△□△_
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Creating Patterns - Create patterns with objects

questions.question: Trace and complete: ☆○☆○☆_
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Complete Patterns by Tracing - Complete visual patterns by tracing or drawing missing elements

questions.question: What comes next? 1, 2, 1, 2, 1, _
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Shape and Number Patterns - Observe, extend and create patterns

questions.question: How many eyes do you have? Look in the mirror and count.
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Identifying Objects - Count body parts, observe surroundings

questions.question: Count the number of red objects in your classroom. Write the number.
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Collecting Objects - Collect and sort objects

questions.question: Look at the picture: There are 4 apples and 2 bananas. Which fruit has MORE?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Observation and Inference - Draw conclusions from pictures

questions.question: In a picture, 3 children like cricket and 2 like football. Which sport is liked more?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Collecting and Interpreting Data - Collect records using pictures and numerals, interpret visual information

questions.question: If today is Monday, what day was yesterday?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Daily-Life Time Vocabulary - Yesterday, today, tomorrow

questions.question: Which day is a holiday? (Monday, Sunday, Wednesday, Friday)
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Special Days - Sunday, holidays, festivals

questions.question: How many days are in a week?
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Days and Months - Recite days and months

questions.question: Name the first month of the year.
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Days and Months (Class 1) - Identify days of the week and months of the year

questions.question: What time is shown on the clock? (Image: clock showing 3:00)
questions.reasoning: Covers subtopic: Analog Clock (Hour, Half-Hour, Quarter) - Tell time to the hour, half-hour, and quarter-hour on an analog clock
